the_good: Core risk enforcement exists and is test-validated in the hardened workspace stack (daily loss guard, exposure/leverage checks, kill-switch behavior, bounded retry, fail-closed wallet preflight).
Execution service includes meaningful institutional primitives: idempotency controls, nonce replay protection, signed-token validation, gateway state machine, and audit event emission.

Backtest automation has strong guardrails for statistical hygiene (walk-forward gating, PF realism thresholding, open-ended timerange warning, atomic writes, history retention, schema migration handling).
Allocation-to-execution linkage is implemented: latest allocation decisions can adjust size and disable strategies before order flow.
Walk-forward framework exists with explicit OOS overlap detection and stability scoring logic.
Monitoring stack is operational and alert delivery path was repaired and validated end-to-end to production webhook ingestion.
Non-env test evidence is solid for key modules (risk, allocator, research integration, walk-forward, backtest parser suites passing).
the_bad: Requested strategy implementations (TrendFollowingBase, MeanReversion, VolatilityBreakout, RegimeClassifier classes) are not present in this codebase as concrete Python strategy classes; they are mostly treated as string identifiers.
No real detect_regime indicator engine (EMA/ADX/ATR computation) is implemented in the reviewed trading code; regime is ingested from markdown text outputs.
Current eligibility quality is poor: latest backtest rows show all tracked strategies ineligible (PF < 1, negative returns, stability failures).
Walk-forward records include runs with all-zero PF/stability values, indicating brittle parsing/execution quality despite framework existence.
Automation is only partially evidenced: cron entries exist, but key logs (backtest/walkforward) are sparse/empty at review time.
Research influence is narrow: allocation currently consumes only regime/news text files; crypto-structure and polymarket are not wired into decision math.
Execution realism remains simplistic (fixed mock slippage/fees in gateway paths, limited liquidity/latency realism, no robust market impact model).
Monitoring still has unresolved active alerts (AgentWorkflowStall, MetricsExporterUnavailable), indicating persistent operational instability.
the_ugly: Critical wiring drift: default execution URL resolves to a non-execution service (:8000/execute returns 404), and default signer endpoint (:9000/sign) is unavailable; this is a direct trade-flow failure risk.
Database path split creates hidden state corruption risk (active DB vs empty shadow DB file). One typo/path mismatch can silently bypass live risk history.
execution_gateway.py contains orphaned duplicate code blocks after runtime section, indicating uncontrolled merge debt and increasing undefined-behavior risk.
metrics_exporter.py has broken method binding patterns; runtime symptom observed as 200 responses with zero-byte metrics payload while alerts show exporter unavailable.
Multiple parallel risk/execution stacks coexist (workspace stack vs memory stack) with overlapping responsibilities and inconsistent trust boundaries.
Hardcoded defaults and weak operational secrets posture remain in critical paths (test-style fallback API keys/URLs and permissive defaults), which would fail institutional security review.
Risk-lock dominance currently drives allocation outputs to zero repeatedly, implying system is often effectively non-trading while automation still reports activity.
Architecture has single points of failure across local files, local SQLite, and local process endpoints with limited resilience/failover design.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: Execution endpoint/signer miswiring (default RPC target mismatch and signer absent)
critical_risks_ranked.impact: Orders can be rejected or misrouted at runtime, causing either complete outage or uncontrolled fallback behavior.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: Strategy and regime engine gap vs declared design (missing concrete strategy/regime classifier implementations)
critical_risks_ranked.impact: Desk behavior depends on external labels/text artifacts rather than auditable model logic, failing model governance standards.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: Telemetry integrity failure (metrics exporter serving empty payload with active exporter-unavailable alerts)
critical_risks_ranked.impact: Risk committee and on-call visibility are impaired; silent degradation can persist undetected.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: State fragmentation from duplicate DB paths and duplicate execution/risk subsystems
critical_risks_ranked.impact: Inconsistent decisions, audit gaps, and irreproducible incidents across environments.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: Backtest/eligibility underperformance (recent PF<1 and negative returns across core strategies)
critical_risks_ranked.impact: No robust statistical basis for capital deployment under institutional hurdle rates.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: Execution realism deficit (mock fills, simplistic slippage/fees, weak liquidity/latency modeling)
critical_risks_ranked.impact: PnL expectations and drawdown controls can be materially overstated vs live execution.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: Hardcoded security/operational defaults in critical paths
critical_risks_ranked.impact: Credential and control-plane hygiene would fail formal infosec and operational risk audits.

critical_risks_ranked.risk: Automation observability gap (cron present, but weak log evidence for some loops)
critical_risks_ranked.impact: Operational confidence reduced; harder to prove deterministic daily learning-loop completion.

recommendations_priority_order: P0 - Immediate Fix: Unify runtime wiring (single canonical DB path, explicit execution/signer endpoints, startup self-check hard fail on endpoint mismatch, remove orphaned duplicate gateway code, restore valid metrics payloads).

P1 - High Impact Upgrade: Implement real strategy/regime engine layer (concrete strategy classes, deterministic EMA/ADX/ATR regime computation, explicit model registry/versioning, and direct integration tests from signal -> allocation -> execution).

P2 - Structural Improvement: Raise realism/governance (liquidity/latency/impact model, robust OHLCV integrity checks, structural+polymarket factor ingestion, centralized config/secrets, and resilience patterns for process/DB/alerting SPOFs).
honest_assessment_summary: This is not institutional-ready. You have a strong base of risk-control intent and some genuinely solid hardening work, but the desk still behaves like a structured prototype with critical wiring drift, missing declared strategy/regime implementations, telemetry integrity problems, and fragmented architecture. A hedge fund risk committee would block Phase 2 promotion until execution-path correctness, model governance, and observability integrity are made deterministic and auditable.
